notes: Cold conditions. Slightly faster pace possible. Risk of respiratory discomfort. Warm up thoroughly.
Near-ideal running conditions. No adjustment needed.
Ideal racing conditions. Performance benchmarks are most accurate here.
Slightly warm. Minor performance reduction. Stay hydrated.
Warm. Slow easy runs by 3%. Heart rate will run 4-5 bpm higher at same effort. Increase fluid intake.
Hot. Reduce intensity. Consider shortening workouts. REI will appear lower — normal in heat.
Hot and humid. Significant performance reduction. Aerobic efficiency (AE) will worsen — not a fitness regression, just heat stress.
Very hot. Reduce volume. HR 8-12 bpm above normal at same effort is expected. If HR drift (HRD) is >10%, cut the run short.
Dangerous heat. Consider moving workout indoors or to early morning. Any metrics collected in these conditions should be excluded from baseline calculations.
High humidity effect is additive to temperature. Body cannot cool efficiently via sweat evaporation. Treat AE degradation as environmental, not a fitness decline.
